message.content:
<teammate-message teammate_id="team-lead" summary="Keep all commits, they're from new Luca">
Kent — keep everything. The committed work is from the NEW Luca (the respawned one), not the old one. These commits are exactly what we wanted — small, incremental, committed at each passing step. The old Luca's uncommitted work was already removed before the new Luca started.

No reverts needed. The new Luca is building correctly. He should be working on integration tests (step 4) next — that's where your TDD coaching is most valuable. Please review what he's building and guide the test infrastructure.
</teammate-message>

content.text:
Understood -- all commits are good, no reverts needed. The uncommitted work in the working tree is Luca's active in-progress work on Task 5. My job is to review and guide as he builds.

The current state is clear:
- **Committed through `9af4522`**: Everything up to HTMX indicator CSS and aria-live region
- **Uncommitted (in progress)**: Domain types, registration service, repository, migration, GET /register route + test, template

All 20 tests pass, all quality gates clean. The `GET /register` integration test is the first proper outside-in test for Task 5, and it's green. The next TDD step is a failing integration test for `POST /register`.

Let me send guidance directly to Luca on the next steps.

input.content:
Kent Beck here. I've reviewed all your current work on Task 5 and it's excellent. All 20 tests pass, quality gates clean. The outside-in approach with the `GET /register` integration test driving the template and route is exactly right.

**Immediate priority**: Commit the current green state. You have substantial uncommitted work (domain types, service, repository, migration, GET /register route + test, template). All of it is green. Commit it now before doing anything else. We lost code once before -- never again.

**After committing, here's the next TDD cycle**:

### Failing Test #1: POST /register happy path

Write this integration test in `tests/api/register.rs`:

```rust
#[tokio::test]
async fn post_register_with_valid_data_redirects() {
    let app = spawn_app().await;
    let client = reqwest::Client::builder()
        .redirect(reqwest::redirect::Policy::none())
        .build()
        .unwrap();

    let response = client
        .post(format!("{}/register", &app.address))
        .header("content-type", "application/x-www-form-urlencoded")
        .body("email=test%40example.com&password=securepassword123&password_confirmation=securepassword123")
        .send()
        .await
        .expect("Failed to execute request");

    assert_eq!(response.status().as_u16(), 303, "Should redirect after successful registration");
    // For now, redirect to "/" -- we'll refine the target when session infrastructure is in place
}
```

This test will fail because there's no POST handler for `/register` yet. That's your red step. Then make it pass with the minimum code -- wire up `post_register` in `routes/register.rs` that calls `registration::register_user` and returns a 303 redirect on success.

**Key design points for the POST handler**:
- Accept `Form<RegisterFormData>` (Axum form extractor)
- On success: 303 redirect (POST-redirect-GET pattern)
- On validation error: 422 with the registration template re-rendered showing errors
- On duplicate email: 422 with the generic error message (account enumeration prevention per US-1)

Write the test, watch it fail, make it pass with the simplest thing, commit. Then we'll add the error case tests one by one.

**Don't forget**: `DATABASE_URL` must be set for `sqlx::query!` macros to compile. The migration runs automatically in `Application::build()`, so the test database will have the users table.
input.summary: TDD guidance: commit now, then write POST /register test

content.text: New commit `13e8dc5` landed -- "Add registration page template, auth form CSS, and route". And there's more active work: `.sqlx` query cache files (for offline mode/CI), CI workflow changes, and modifications to the register route and test. Luca is moving fast and committing. Let me review what's been committed and what's still in progress.

input.description: Show what was in the latest commit

content.content:
commit 13e8dc50e73f6c42ffaf41e3f148e076bfb77034
Good "git" signature for john@johnwilger.com with ED25519 key SHA256:Fa/i+mjuGYJd8KbzsXhV2I87PNZ8q9S/k8BCW2ayIr0
Author: John Wilger <john@johnwilger.com>
Date:   Sun Feb 8 12:51:40 2026 -0800

    Add registration page template, auth form CSS, and route
    
    Registration form (GET /register) with:
    - Email, password, password confirmation fields with visible labels
    - autocomplete="new-password" on both password fields (US-1)
    - Conditional aria-describedby and aria-invalid for validation errors
    - General error message area with role="alert"
    - "Create account" submit button (action words per team convention)
    - Link to login page in footer
    
    Auth form CSS component (.auth-form) with:
    - Centered, max-width 24rem layout
    - Vertical field stacking with consistent spacing tokens
    - :has([aria-invalid]) for parent-based error border styling
    - Error message and general alert styling using semantic colors
    - Full-width submit button
    
    Integration test verifies form structure, labels, autocomplete
    attributes, and absence of error states on initial load.
    
    Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

    75 
    76 #[tokio::test]
    77 async fn post_register_with_valid_data_redirects() {
    78     let app = spawn_app().await;
    79 
    80     let client = reqwest::Client::builder()
    81         .redirect(reqwest::redirect::Policy::none())
    82         .build()
    83         .unwrap();
    84 
    85     let response = client
    86         .post(format!("{}/register", &app.address))
    87         .form(&[
    88             ("email", "test@example.com"),
    89             ("password", "securepassword123"),
    90             ("password_confirmation", "securepassword123"),
    91         ])
    92         .send()
    93         .await
    94         .expect("Failed to execute request");
    95 
    96     assert_eq!(
    97         response.status().as_u16(),
    98         303,
    99         "Expected 303 See Other redirect after successful registration"
   100     );
   101 }
   102 
   103 #[tokio::test]
   104 async fn post_register_persists_user_in_database() {
   105     let app = spawn_app().await;
   106 
   107     let client = reqwest::Client::builder()
   108         .redirect(reqwest::redirect::Policy::none())
   109         .build()
   110         .unwrap();
   111 
   112     client
   113         .post(format!("{}/register", &app.address))
   114         .form(&[
   115             ("email", "persist@example.com"),
   116             ("password", "securepassword123"),
   117             ("password_confirmation", "securepassword123"),
   118         ])
   119         .send()
   120         .await
   121         .expect("Failed to execute request");
   122 
   123     let saved = sqlx::query!(
   124         "SELECT email, password_hash FROM users WHERE email = $1",
   125         "persist@example.com"
   126     )
   127     .fetch_one(&app.pool)
   128     .await
   129     .expect("Failed to fetch saved user");
   130 
   131     assert_eq!(saved.email, "persist@example.com");
   132     assert!(
   133         saved.password_hash.starts_with("$argon2"),
   134         "Password should be hashed with Argon2"
   135     );
   136 }
   137 
   138 #[tokio::test]
   139 async fn post_register_returns_422_when_email_is_empty() {
   140     let app = spawn_app().await;
   141 
   142     let client = reqwest::Client::new();
   143     let response = client
   144         .post(format!("{}/register", &app.address))
   145         .form(&[
   146             ("email", ""),
   147             ("password", "securepassword123"),
   148             ("password_confirmation", "securepassword123"),
   149         ])
   150         .send()
   151         .await
   152         .expect("Failed to execute request");
   153 
   154     assert_eq!(response.status().as_u16(), 422);
   155     let body = response.text().await.unwrap();
   156     assert!(
   157         body.contains("Enter your email address"),
   158         "Should show email error message"
   159     );
   160 }
   161 
   162 #[tokio::test]
   163 async fn post_register_returns_422_when_email_is_invalid() {
   164     let app = spawn_app().await;
   165 
   166     let client = reqwest::Client::new();
   167     let response = client
   168         .post(format!("{}/register", &app.address))
   169         .form(&[
   170             ("email", "notanemail"),
   171             ("password", "securepassword123"),
   172             ("password_confirmation", "securepassword123"),
   173         ])
   174         .send()
   175         .await
   176         .expect("Failed to execute request");
   177 
   178     assert_eq!(response.status().as_u16(), 422);
   179     let body = response.text().await.unwrap();
   180     assert!(
   181         body.contains("name@example.com"),
   182         "Should show email format hint"
   183     );
   184 }
   185 
   186 #[tokio::test]
   187 async fn post_register_returns_422_when_password_too_short() {
   188     let app = spawn_app().await;
   189 
   190     let client = reqwest::Client::new();
   191     let response = client
   192         .post(format!("{}/register", &app.address))
   193         .form(&[
   194             ("email", "test@example.com"),
   195             ("password", "short"),
   196             ("password_confirmation", "short"),
   197         ])
   198         .send()
   199         .await
   200         .expect("Failed to execute request");
   201 
   202     assert_eq!(response.status().as_u16(), 422);
   203     let body = response.text().await.unwrap();
   204     assert!(
   205         body.contains("at least 8 characters"),
   206         "Should show password length error"
   207     );
   208 }
   209 
   210 #[tokio::test]
   211 async fn post_register_returns_422_when_passwords_dont_match() {
   212     let app = spawn_app().await;
   213 
   214     let client = reqwest::Client::new();
   215     let response = client
   216         .post(format!("{}/register", &app.address))
   217         .form(&[
   218             ("email", "test@example.com"),
   219             ("password", "securepassword123"),
   220             ("password_confirmation", "differentpassword"),
   221         ])
   222         .send()
   223         .await
   224         .expect("Failed to execute request");
   225 
   226     assert_eq!(response.status().as_u16(), 422);
   227     let body = response.text().await.unwrap();
   228     assert!(
   229         body.contains("Passwords do not match"),
   230         "Should show password mismatch error"
   231     );
   232 }
   233 
   234 #[tokio::test]
   235 async fn post_register_returns_422_for_duplicate_email_without_leaking_info() {
   236     let app = spawn_app().await;
   237 
   238     let client = reqwest::Client::builder()
   239         .redirect(reqwest::redirect::Policy::none())
   240         .build()
   241         .unwrap();
   242 
   243     // Register the first user
   244     client
   245         .post(format!("{}/register", &app.address))
   246         .form(&[
   247             ("email", "duplicate@example.com"),
   248             ("password", "securepassword123"),
   249             ("password_confirmation", "securepassword123"),
   250         ])
   251         .send()
   252         .await
   253         .expect("Failed to execute request");
   254 
   255     // Try to register with the same email
   256     let response = client
   257         .post(format!("{}/register", &app.address))
   258         .form(&[
   259             ("email", "duplicate@example.com"),
   260             ("password", "anotherpassword123"),
   261             ("password_confirmation", "anotherpassword123"),
   262         ])
   263         .send()
   264         .await
   265         .expect("Failed to execute request");
   266 
   267     assert_eq!(response.status().as_u16(), 422);
   268     let body = response.text().await.unwrap();
   269     // Must NOT reveal that the email exists (account enumeration prevention)
   270     assert!(
   271         !body.contains("already exists"),
   272         "Should not reveal email existence"
   273     );
   274     assert!(
   275         body.contains("signing in"),
   276         "Should suggest signing in as an alternative"
   277     );
   278 }
   279 
   280 #[tokio::test]
   281 async fn post_register_preserves_email_on_validation_error() {
   282     let app = spawn_app().await;
   283 
   284     let client = reqwest::Client::new();
   285     let response = client
   286         .post(format!("{}/register", &app.address))
   287         .form(&[
   288             ("email", "test@example.com"),
   289             ("password", "short"),
   290             ("password_confirmation", "short"),
   291         ])
   292         .send()
   293         .await
   294         .expect("Failed to execute request");
   295 
   296     let body = response.text().await.unwrap();
   297     assert!(
   298         body.contains("test@example.com"),
   299         "Email should be preserved in the form on error"
   300     );
   301 }

    76 #[tokio::test]
    77 async fn post_register_with_valid_data_redirects() {
    78     let app = spawn_app().await;
    79 
    80     let client = reqwest::Client::builder()

    94         .expect("Failed to execute request");
    95

   103 #[tokio::test]
   104 async fn post_register_persists_user_in_database() {
   105     let app = spawn_app().await;
   106 
   107     let client = reqwest::Client::builder()

   119         .send()
   120         .await
   121         .expect("Failed to execute request");
   122 
   123     let saved = sqlx::query!(
   124         "SELECT email, password_hash FROM users WHERE email = $1",
   125         "persist@example.com"
   126     )
   127     .fetch_one(&app.pool)

   210 #[tokio::test]
   211 async fn post_register_returns_422_when_passwords_dont_match() {
   212     let app = spawn_app().await;
   213 
   214     let client = reqwest::Client::new();
   215     let response = client
   216         .post(format!("{}/register", &app.address))
   217         .form(&[
   218             ("email", "test@example.com"),
   219             ("password", "securepassword123"),
   220             ("password_confirmation", "differentpassword"),
   221         ])
   222         .send()
   223         .await
   224         .expect("Failed to execute request");
   225 
   226     assert_eq!(response.status().as_u16(), 422);
   227     let body = response.text().await.unwrap();
   228     assert!(

   233 
   234 #[tokio::test]
   235 async fn post_register_returns_422_for_duplicate_email_without_leaking_info() {
   236     let app = spawn_app().await;
   237 
   238     let client = reqwest::Client::builder()
   239         .redirect(reqwest::redirect::Policy::none())
   240         .build()
   241         .unwrap();
   242 
   243     // Register the first user
   244     client
   245         .post(format!("{}/register", &app.address))
   246         .form(&[
   247             ("email", "duplicate@example.com"),
   248             ("password", "securepassword123"),
   249             ("password_confirmation", "securepassword123"),
   250         ])
   251         .send()
   252         .await
   253         .expect("Failed to execute request");
   254 
   255     // Try to register with the same email
   256     let response = client
   257         .post(format!("{}/register", &app.address))
   258         .form(&[
   259             ("email", "duplicate@example.com"),
   260             ("password", "anotherpassword123"),
   261             ("password_confirmation", "anotherpassword123"),
   262         ])
   263         .send()
   264         .await
   265         .expect("Failed to execute request");
   266 
   267     assert_eq!(response.status().as_u16(), 422);
   268     let body = response.text().await.unwrap();
   269     // Must NOT reveal that the email exists (account enumeration prevention)
   270     assert!(
   271         !body.contains("already exists"),
   272         "Should not reveal email existence"
   273     );
   274     assert!(
   275         body.contains("signing in"),
   276         "Should suggest signing in as an alternative"
   277     );
   278 }
   279 
   280 #[tokio::test]
   281 async fn post_register_preserves_email_on_validation_error() {

   290             ("password_confirmation", "short"),
   291         ])
   292         .send()
   293         .await
   294         .expect("Failed to execute request");
   295 
   296     let body = response.text().await.unwrap();
   297     assert!(
   298         body.contains("test@example.com"),
   299         "Email should be preserved in the form on error"
   300     );
   301 }

content.text:
A compile error -- `Redirect::see_other` doesn't exist in Axum 0.8. The compiler helpfully suggests the available methods. In Axum 0.8, the redirect constructors are `to` (303), `temporary` (307), and `permanent` (308). `Redirect::to()` is actually a 303 See Other, which is exactly what we want for POST-redirect-GET.

Let me check the Axum docs to confirm, then provide guidance.
Confirmed: `Redirect::to()` in Axum 0.8 produces a 303 See Other. That's exactly what we need. Luca just needs to change `Redirect::see_other("/login")` to `Redirect::to("/login")`.

input.content:
Kent here. Quick compile fix needed: `Redirect::see_other` doesn't exist in Axum 0.8. Use `Redirect::to("/login")` instead -- it produces a 303 See Other, which is exactly the POST-redirect-GET behavior we want.

In `src/routes/register.rs` line 51, change:
```rust
Ok(_user_id) => Ok(Redirect::see_other("/login").into_response()),
```
to:
```rust
Ok(_user_id) => Ok(Redirect::to("/login").into_response()),
```

I've confirmed from the Axum 0.8 source: `Redirect::to()` -> 303 See Other, `Redirect::temporary()` -> 307, `Redirect::permanent()` -> 308.

The rest of your code looks excellent. The integration tests are comprehensive -- happy path, database persistence verification, all validation error variants, duplicate email with account enumeration prevention, and email preservation on error. This is thorough TDD. 

One observation on the tests: `post_register_persists_user_in_database` directly queries the database to verify the user was saved with an Argon2 hash. That's a good "verify the side effect" pattern -- the test doesn't just check the HTTP response, it checks that the system actually did what it was supposed to do.

After fixing the compile error, run the tests and commit as soon as green.

routing.summary: Compile fix: Redirect::to not see_other in Axum 0.8
routing.content:
Kent here. Quick compile fix needed: `Redirect::see_other` doesn't exist in Axum 0.8. Use `Redirect::to("/login")` instead -- it produces a 303 See Other, which is exactly the POST-redirect-GET behavior we want.

In `src/routes/register.rs` line 51, change:
```rust
Ok(_user_id) => Ok(Redirect::see_other("/login").into_response()),
```
to:
```rust
Ok(_user_id) => Ok(Redirect::to("/login").into_response()),
```

I've confirmed from the Axum 0.8 source: `Redirect::to()` -> 303 See Other, `Redirect::temporary()` -> 307, `Redirect::permanent()` -> 308.

The rest of your code looks excellent. The integration tests are comprehensive -- happy path, database persistence verification, all validation error variants, duplicate email with account enumeration prevention, and email preservation on error. This is thorough TDD. 

One observation on the tests: `post_register_persists_user_in_database` directly queries the database to verify the user was saved with an Argon2 hash. That's a good "verify the side effect" pattern -- the test doesn't just check the HTTP response, it checks that the system actually did what it was supposed to do.

After fixing the compile error, run the tests and commit as soon as green.

content.text:
I've identified and communicated the compile fix to Luca. The issue is straightforward -- Axum 0.8 renamed `see_other` to `to` for the 303 redirect constructor. The rest of the POST /register implementation looks very well done:

- **8 integration tests** covering: GET form rendering, POST happy path with redirect, POST database persistence, empty email, invalid email, short password, mismatched passwords, duplicate email (with account enumeration prevention), and email preservation on validation errors
- **User-friendly error messages** mapped from domain errors (e.g., `PasswordError::TooShort` -> "Your password needs at least 8 characters")
- **Duplicate email handling** uses the generic message from US-1: "Unable to create account. If you already have an account, try signing in." with a link -- no account enumeration leakage
- **Form state preservation** -- email value is retained when validation fails

I'm monitoring for Luca's fix and the next commit.
